grounding_note: Third batch for the SSI/SSD routing family.

scenarios.opening_query: The disability examiner scheduled me for their doctor and then used that one visit to deny everything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my SSDI claim — 26 years as a hospital orderly before my knees failed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — 26 years as a hospital orderly before my knees gave out.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which program's consultative-exam denial is at issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my SSI — I've never had steady work because of my condition.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — my condition kept me from steady work my whole life.
scenarios.vetting_note: CE-denial complaint common to both; program fact routes.

scenarios.opening_query: My twin brother and I both applied for disability with the same condition and got opposite results.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: The difference is the program — he worked years and got SSDI; I couldn't work and applied SSI, where they counted my wife's income against me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the programs — he had work years and got SSDI; mine is SSI and they counted my wife's income against me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which program each application ran under
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We both filed SSDI — mine failed on the date-last-insured rule because I stopped working earlier than he did.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Both SSDI — mine failed the date-last-insured rule since I stopped working earlier.
scenarios.vetting_note: Twin framing makes the program mechanics vivid and disclosable.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm on dialysis three days a week and they still say I'm not disabled enough. I need a real advocate.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The claim rides on my own record — 20 years of transit-authority wages before the kidneys failed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's on my record — 20 years with the transit authority before my kidneys failed.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I have no work quarters — kidney disease since my twenties; it's the needs-based side.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No work quarters — I've been sick since my twenties. Needs-based side.
scenarios.vetting_note: High-severity case where routing still needs the record fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Social Security says my disability ended because I tried a part-time job for a few months.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's SSDI — the fight is my trial-work-period rights, which they miscounted.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — they miscounted my trial-work-period months.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's SSI — the job income pushed me over their monthly limit, and now they say the whole disability 'ended.'
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — the income went over their monthly limit and they act like my disability itself ended.
scenarios.vetting_note: Work-incentive rules differ by program; the disclosure names the applicable rule set.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter turned 18 and the disability office suddenly wants to redo her whole case.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's on childhood SSI — the age-18 redetermination applies the adult SSI standard, and that's the review we're facing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's on childhood SSI — it's the age-18 adult-standard redetermination.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the review is the SSI age-18 redetermination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She receives SSDI as a disabled adult child on her late father's record — the review is about that DAC status.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She gets SSDI as a disabled adult child on her late father's record — the review is that status.
scenarios.vetting_note: Age-18 events hit both programs through different doors; benefit source routes.

scenarios.opening_query: I lost my leg in an accident two years ago and I still can't get approved for anything.
scenarios.hidden_fact: My application is on my work record — 15 years of roofing wages before the accident.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's on my work record — 15 years roofing before the accident.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The accident happened right out of high school — no work history, so it's the SSI application.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It happened right out of high school — no work history, so SSI.

scenarios.opening_query: The office keeps sending my elderly aunt forms she can't understand and threatening to stop her checks.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her check is disability on her own record from her decades of cannery wages — an SSDI continuing review, not the welfare-style paperwork.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her check is SSDI from her cannery decades — it's their continuing review paperwork.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's SSI — the forms are the redetermination wanting her rent receipts and bank statements again.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — the redetermination forms wanting rent receipts and bank statements again.
scenarios.vetting_note: Helper-voice paperwork panic; document types hint but the program fact decides.

scenarios.opening_query: After my stroke I applied for disability and my neighbor with the same thing got approved while I got denied.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: He filed SSDI on his record; my cleaning work was always cash, so mine is SSI — and the denial says my husband's pension makes me 'not needy.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His was SSDI on his record; my cleaning work was cash, so mine's SSI — and they say my husband's pension makes me 'not needy.'
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We both filed SSDI — mine was denied on medical grounds despite the same stroke, and I want that fought.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Both SSDI — mine got denied on medical grounds despite the same stroke.
scenarios.vetting_note: Comparison framing surfaces the program-vs-medical denial distinction.
